preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of debuccalization and extreme lenition, typically occurring in coda positions or as part of glottal reinforcement in specific dialects.

examples.note: Documentation of glottalization in voiced fricatives where the oral gesture is reduced or bypassed in favor of a glottal stop.
Categorized as an extreme stage of weakening where the oral constriction is entirely lost, leaving only the glottal closure gesture.

sources: Phonological research on English coda reduction and debuccalization.
